h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: So the downward arrow is Earth on the cart, not the cart moving down.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The rolling direction is not what fixes the label.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner seemed headed toward simply labeling all arrows by rote with the 'object on cart' formula as directed.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Under the arrow: hand on the cart, pushing right. The rolling-left sentence goes over here, because it says what the cart does, not what is acting on it.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly sorts sentence strips into interaction and motion categories, visibly enacting the new device for distinguishing force from motion.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: slides two sentence strips below the sideways arrow, then sets a blank “not a force label” space to the side ... The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: After L2's confusion about the moving cart, the tutor contrasts the old labeling route with a new, explicit sorting device—sentence strips and a discard space—making the force/motion distinction a public, actionable task.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: slides two sentence strips below the sideways arrow, then sets a blank “not a force label” space to the side ... Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is precise (two concrete strips, one discard space), directly fitted to the learner's motion/force conflation, gives the learner an actionable criterion for testing each statement, and introduces a transferable sorting tool rather than just explaining or completing the answer.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly acknowledges L2's revised framing—that 'the rolling direction is not what fixes the label'—and builds a new interactive sorting task to test and reinforce that revised understanding, rather than continuing the original card-placement-only routine.
